meta.title: Badge
meta.description: Visual indicator for status and quantity. In Wind, Badge surfaces status through intuitive color mapping.

hero.title: Badge
hero.desc: Badge surfaces status or quantity information visually. In Wind, Badge is most often used to communicate status through intuitive color mapping.

usage.title: Usage
usage.desc1: Badge splits into six categories by the type of information it carries.
usage.desc2: Box Badge and Label Badge surface progress or status through color mapping. Count Badge surfaces the total count within a given scope. Beyond these three primary types, Severity Badge, Button Badge, and Text Badge are also used.

items.title: 1. Box Badge
items.desc: Box form. Carries no label inside — communicates information through color alone.

items.title: 2. Contained Badge
items.desc: Label can be Text or Number. Text Type often pairs with an external label.

items.title: 3. Count Badge
items.desc: Used to surface an overall count as a single discrete value.

items.title: 4. Severity Badge
items.desc: Used in Zoomable to mark Zoomable's five defect levels plus an unconfirmed defect — six tiers in total. A Box-form Badge with the defect tier as text inside.

items.title: 5. Button Badge
items.desc: Button Badge executes a specific action on click.

items.title: 6. Text Badge
items.desc: Text Badge executes a specific action on click.

semanticVariants.title: Semantic Variants
semanticVariants.desc: Severity Badge uses six (plus one) colors to express severity tier by tier. Box Badge uses four colors to express progress stages.

contents.title: Contents
contents.desc:
Because each Badge maps information through color, content beyond text is mapped to the color region — both text and color carry the badge's meaning.

Badge content supports a text label inside the container or an Extra Label outside (text-only). For Count Badge and Severity Badge, the inner Text Label is restricted to numbers. When Box Badge uses an Extra Label, the text marks a specific progress stage — it's fixed text, not user-editable.

sizing.title: Sizing
sizing.desc: Badge sizes are Large, Medium, Small — determined by Height. For Contained, Count, and Severity Badges, Height changes drive the size; text length adjustments are validated independently of Height. Width follows either an equal-to-Height rule or a minimum-size + padding rule that auto-fits content.

rules.title: 1. Text Character = 1
rules.desc: For single-character Badges, padding stays constant and the badge follows a fixed Width-to-Height ratio. Auto mode doesn't use a fixed Height.

rules.title: 2. Text Characters > 1
rules.desc: For multi-character text, the same padding values apply consistently. The size stays stable across text-length changes — character count drives only Width within a single line, and the variable-Width rule doesn't extend beyond a single word (Single Words: Medium, Only). Size requires explicit values.

rows.values: To Do, In Progress, In Review, Done
